understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through KA59’s levels by arranging the board so that a pushable object (block) is moved onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+', triggering the engine’s WIN condition within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The game is played on a grid represented both as pixel coordinates ([x,y]) and as a compact semantic_grid where each cell corresponds to 3 pixels. MOVE actions attempt to shift the currently selected piece (P) by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, but movement can be blocked by walls/boundaries (#) or other objects; the movement.*_blocked flags are only hints and do not guarantee failure. If a pushable block (B) is immediately adjacent in the movement direction, a successful move can push the block along with the selected piece, provided space allows. Some levels contain multiple selectable pieces (S); using CLICK with an exact object pixel position swaps control to that selectable, changing which piece responds to subsequent MOVE actions.
